programme.title: Griddle Nation
programme.desc: Spice lovers rejoice as the griddle ignites with bold flavor. From smoky peppers to bright heat and layered seasoning, this exploration celebrates the art of balancing fire and flavor. Home cooks experiment with spice to create dishes that bring warmth and excitement to every bite.

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish host Micah Trainor tries her hand at an Italian original - Veal Piccata! Lemony, buttery, capERS-y veal melts in the mouth after being pounded supERS-thin and seared to perfection. Poppy Sandler's sideways switch-up is a Caper Almond Crusted Halibut, sizzled and baked until gorgeously golden-brown and flaky, then garnished with chives and pea shoots. Next, Missy Hui goes Greek with her vegetarian Avgolemono, a silky-smooth citrusy soup starring lemon and egg, garnished with orzo and shimeji mushrooms. Tiera Singh ties it all together with her Sumac Veal Sweetbreads with a rough chopped caperberry-olive tapenade - browned veal sweetbreads are sprinkled with tart sumac and laid on a bed of tapenade, then drizzled with a sweet 'n' sour pomegranate molasses vinaigrette.

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: First up on this episode of Up the Dish is Poppy Sandler's Borscht straight from the Eastern Bloc! A mix of roasted and simmered beets adds complexity to the creamy soup - and don't forget that vivid burgundy hue. Tiera Singh swaps soup for a sandwich with her Beet-Stained Trout Gravlax, pairing the eye-catching seafood with zesty lemon-dill cream cheese and rye pumpernickel bread. Next, Micah Trainor mixes up a vegetarian Roasted Beet Salad paired with crackly Seed Brittle and a simple sweet dressing; it's a crunchy, earthy delight! Missy Hui's three-layer Red Beet Velvet Cake is a true triple threat. Red beet puree gives this cake its vermillion colour - and pairing it with tangy goat cheese icing... well, it just makes sense!

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: In this episode of Up the Dish, host Micah Trainor takes a stab at Cassoulet with buttery white beans, duck leg confit, and flavourful French sausage - and the result is mouth-wateringly good! The pressure's on, but Tiera Singh doesn't let up with her Chinese Long Bean Stir Fry. Succulent sausage, delicious duck breast, and flavorful vegetables come together for a truly sumptuous dish! For her vegetarian variant, Missy Hui must move things up a notch, but how? Agedashi Tofu! Breaded crispy semi-firm tofu is doused in a dashi, gochujang, and mirin mixture for the ultimate umami mouthful. Poppy Sandler goes above and beyond with her Chocolate-crusted Elk Loin, seared and drizzled with a decadent Coffee- Blueberry Demi-Glace - sound strange? Tastes terrific!

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: How do you make any lasagna recipe better? Ask Tiera Singh to take it up, of course! Her 'Nonna-style' bechamel and Bolognese-based Lasagna is topped with melty mozzarella for a luscious and luxuriant dish. Tiera tosses it over to Poppy Sandler, whose sideways Seafood Lasagna mixes up mussels, shrimp, and a briny bechamel sauce - yum! Next, Micah Trainor makes a dressed-up vegetarian version of the classic - her Zucchini-Mushroom Lasagna Rolls with tangy sundried tomatoes, savoury ricotta cheese, and earthy mushroom duxelles is simply superlative. Missy Hui hits the sweet spot with her Chocolate Raspberry Dessert Lasagna; layers of rich white chocolate, thick raspberry coulis, and velvety mascarpone cheese come together for a dessert that's in the stratosphere!

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: Host Poppy Sandler kicks off Up the Dish with her version of mom's spaghetti - a scrumptious Spaghetti Pomodoro that takes it to the top! Next up to the plate is Missy Hui, who hits it out of the park with her heavenly Bun Viet, a sweet and sour vermicelli noodle bowl and citrusy lemongrass grilled pork combo. Then, Tiera Singh takes up the challenge with her vegetarian Miso Ramen - an umami flavour bomb of miso, mirin, and even more noodle goodness. But there's nowhere to go but up with Micah's Lobster Cantonese Chow Mein, an elegantly upscaled rendition of Chinese noodles with bright ginger, tender gai lan, and crisp chow mein, all coated in an exquisite black bean sauce.

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: Get ready for a not-so-raw deal in this episode of Up the Dish! Host Poppy Sandler sets things in motion and slices up a simple, elegant Sashimi with tangy Ponzu and tart Wasabi Mayo on the side. Missy Hui gets carnivorous with Lamb Carpaccio and Dukkah - delicate raw lamb and a Middle Eastern-style dip served with some delicious pita! But wait, what's a vegetarian to do with all this meat? Tiera Singh's got the answer - island-style Poke Bowls, with fruit instead of fish. Refreshing watermelon is marinated in a savoury, soy-based sauce and served with rice. For the finale, Micah Trainor Up('s) the Dish with her Scallop Tartare and Beet Chips, combining raw scallops with ultra-decadent truffle oil and sour cream.

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Renowned Chef Michael Bonacini takes viewers on a culinary tour of the Italian region of Trentino-Alto Adige without leaving his own kitchen.

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Charming host Chef Michael Bonacini explores the mountainous region of Basilicata with a feast fit for any celebration. Beginning with an antipasto that caters to the region's southern heat, Michael stuffs crunchy sweet peppers with ripe tomatoes, savoury anchovies, and fresh oregano. Moving on to the primo, Michael cooks up a creamy Vermicelli Frittata. Using leftover pasta, he combines eggs with cheese, anchovies, and chili flakes for extra flavour to make a classic Basilicata dish. Michael cooks up Pollo Alla Potentina, a traditional meal from the Feast of Saint Gerard. This hearty chicken stew is sure to heat up your core, using hot peppers as the base of the meal. For his final dish, Michael combines the rich tastes of pomegranate, walnuts, and dark chocolate to create his unique Sweet Wheat Bowl.

programme.title: Fish the Dish
programme.desc: Seafood loving chef Spencer Watts gets casual and formal with monkfish and clam recipes that feature some of his favorite libations - beer and wine.

programme.title: Fish the Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts is making a cookbook all about fish. Here he cooks up some swordfish and scallop recipes that feature some of his favorite nuts.

programme.title: Fish the Dish
programme.desc: Fish loves spice and Chef Spencer Watts loves fish so he brings the delicious world of spice to halibut and lobster along with some cooling sides.

programme.title: Fish the Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts shares some of his favorite mackerel and mussel recipes that highlight aromatic, vibrant and fresh herbs.

programme.title: Griddle Nation
programme.desc: Comfort and indulgence meet in the griddle’s glow as cheese turns to molten perfection. From timeless classics to inventive creations, these dishes stretch, crisp, and bubble, proving everything tastes better with a little golden melt.

programme.title: Griddle Nation
programme.desc: Spice lovers rejoice as the griddle ignites with bold flavor. From smoky peppers to bright heat and layered seasoning, this exploration celebrates the art of balancing fire and flavor. Home cooks experiment with spice to create dishes that bring warmth and excitement to every bite.

programme.title: Griddle Nation
programme.desc: Imagination meets the warmth of the griddle in dishes designed for kids’ tastes. Playful shapes, bright flavors, and familiar favorites come together to charm even the pickiest palates with every bite.

programme.title: The Latin Kitchen
programme.desc: Bring the heat to your kitchen with Chef Luis Valenzuela as he shows us the diversity of chilies in four different recipes.

programme.title: Cook the Books
programme.desc: Chef-host Shahir Massoud tries out recipes from an award-winning cookbook: Chinese-ish by Joanna Hu and Rosheen Kaul! Follow along as Shahir makes fried rice fit for a billionaire, Australian-inspired shrimp toast, comforting chicken soup, and much more!

programme.title: Cook the Books
programme.desc: Chef-host Shahir Massoud tries out recipes from another award-winning cookbook: Pasta Every Day by Meryl Feinstein! Follow along as Shahir demonstrates how to make a variety of pasta shapes, like cavatelli, farfalle, and gnocchi, complete with delicious sauces!

programme.title: One BIG Recipe
programme.desc: When planning a multi-course dinner for six, we all think 'go with the classics' and so does sassy chef-host Kim Lallouz. For her, however, it's Classics... with a Twist! Use the One big Recipe and cook along as Bread and Butter gets a smoke, honey, herb, and black sea salt makeover. Next, Shrimp Cocktail is reinvented with a horseradish kick in her Apricot Ketchup. Then, it's Potato Pave as a standalone course, garnished with tangy tarragon crÃ¨me fraiche and briny salmon roe. Prime Rib is as classic as it comes, but with a sous-vide twist and truffle-spiked Pea and Lardon salad, plan to drop everything so you can twist along! For a sweet ending, dress up dessert from your favourite bakery with a cool trick for making easy lavender ice cream. Dinner is love, and you will love this dinner!

programme.title: One BIG Recipe
programme.desc: Spring is in the air for this multi-course dinner One Big Recipe with engaging Chef Kimberly Lallouz. First up, it's Scallop Crudo, and it's peachy! Literally! Lamb Shanks braised with olives and sun-dried tomatoes are next, as an appetizer ragout, lovingly stuffed into pillowy, perfect mini-Yorkshire Puddings. Next, verdant spears of asparagus appear, topped with creamy, crunchy, soft-cooked egg Sauce Gribiche. Halibut is the main event, pan-roasted steaks are served over king trumpet Dill Pesto Mushrooms and finished with many melty coins of punchy Pink Peppercorn Compound Butter. The entire dinner is laid out in the signature One Big Recipe format, easy to follow, easy to make and guaranteed to impress!

programme.title: Griddle Nation
programme.desc: Comfort and indulgence meet in the griddle’s glow as cheese turns to molten perfection. From timeless classics to inventive creations, these dishes stretch, crisp, and bubble, proving everything tastes better with a little golden melt.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern gives us a little surprise on the inside by baking up tasty filled goodies.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Make hosting your next shower a cinch with Jessica McGovern's simple and super cute recipes - like tiny Oatmeal Cookie Cheesecake Cups!

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Flowers aren't just for smelling - they're great for baking too! Host Jessica McGovern shows us how to bake some tasty desserts with a floral twist.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern shows us the versatility of eggs by baking up some tasty, egg-centric desserts like CrÃ¨me Brulee and Lemon Meringue Pie.

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: For this "N" challenge, Chef Lauren Gulyas works with noodles and nectarines. For her first dish, Lauren cooks up a breaded nectarine and mozzarella stack. Talking technique, she shows us how to cut nectarines into rounds. She does the same with some soft mozzarella and then walks us through the art of a balsamic reduction. For the noodles, Chef Lauren puts an elegant twist on your childhood favourite with a lobster mac and cheese. She spares no flavours, adding fried bacon, garlic, cayenne pepper, mustard powder, and even dry vermouth. This is no ordinary noodle dish. But now for the real challenge! Chef Lauren works with the noodles and nectarines.

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: In this episode, Chef Robert Jewell explores recipes using two very different ingredients that start with the letter "O," oats and okra. For his first dish, Robert uses okra in a classic cornmeal cake recipe, but bumps up the flavour factor with spicy jalapenos. Robert balances the heat of the jalapeno with a tart green tomato and crunchy red onion salsa. In his next recipe, Robert keeps the heat, but switches up his recipes for a curry-spiced oat pancake. This Indian inspired dish needs something to cool it down and Robert knows exactly what that is - sweet, silky applesauce! He's up for the challenge when it comes to combining okra and oats into one dish, and the results are tasty!

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: Culinary expert Theresa Visintin picks the letter "B" and must combine beef with buttermilk for her challenge. But first, she works with the ingredients separately. Silky buttermilk lends itself perfectly to Theresa's battered blooming onion recipe. Using the biggest onion she can find, Theresa skillfully slices from the root down, ensuring it will bloom into a beautiful flower. She coats the Vidalia onion in egg, buttermilk and smoky spiced flour, before deep frying it. Next up, it's time for beef to get the Theresa treatment, as she prepares a crowd favourite- smoky, juicy, fall-off-thebone baked beef brisket. With the perfect balance of sweet and spicy, this meal will make you look forward to leftovers...if there are any! Now for the challenge - Theresa skillfully blends beef with buttermilk in a recipe that will tempt your taste buds.

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: After picking "M" for this episode's challenge, Chef Lauren Gulyas decides to make meal magic using maple and mahi-mahi. She starts by combining chestnut and quinoa to create a mouth-watering medley, rich in flavour and texture. Chef Lauren adds shiitake mushrooms and drizzles the dish with a balsamic vinegar, maple syrup and rosemary dressing. Next up, she steams Mahi-mahi with red chilies and lemongrass, this savoury spice is a heavenly combination with an invigorating aroma. For the challenge, Chef Lauren seamlessly introduces both maple and mahimahi into one flavourful fusion.

programme.desc: Celebrity Chef Michael Bonacini prepares traditional dishes from the Northern Italian region, Lombardy.

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Home to popular Italian dishes like Tiramisu, Chef Michael Bonacini explores cuisine from Italy's Veneto region.

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy

programme.title: One BIG Recipe
programme.desc: When planning a multi-course dinner for six, we all think 'go with the classics' and so does sassy chef-host Kim Lallouz. For her, however, it's Classics... with a Twist! Use the One big Recipe and cook along as Bread and Butter gets a smoke, honey, herb, and black sea salt makeover. Next, Shrimp Cocktail is reinvented with a horseradish kick in her Apricot Ketchup. Then, it's Potato Pave as a standalone course, garnished with tangy tarragon crÃ¨me fraiche and briny salmon roe. Prime Rib is as classic as it comes, but with a sous-vide twist and truffle-spiked Pea and Lardon salad, plan to drop everything so you can twist along! For a sweet ending, dress up dessert from your favourite bakery with a cool trick for making easy lavender ice cream. Dinner is love, and you will love this dinner!

programme.title: One BIG Recipe
programme.desc: Spring is in the air for this multi-course dinner One Big Recipe with engaging Chef Kimberly Lallouz. First up, it's Scallop Crudo, and it's peachy! Literally! Lamb Shanks braised with olives and sun-dried tomatoes are next, as an appetizer ragout, lovingly stuffed into pillowy, perfect mini-Yorkshire Puddings. Next, verdant spears of asparagus appear, topped with creamy, crunchy, soft-cooked egg Sauce Gribiche. Halibut is the main event, pan-roasted steaks are served over king trumpet Dill Pesto Mushrooms and finished with many melty coins of punchy Pink Peppercorn Compound Butter. The entire dinner is laid out in the signature One Big Recipe format, easy to follow, easy to make and guaranteed to impress!

programme.desc: Escuchar la voz de Dios parecería una de las cosas más difíciles de hacer; ¿Cómo podemos entonar nuestro oído a su Espíritu Santo?

programme.title: Lo sobrenatural ahora
programme.desc: El Apóstol Guillermo Maldonado demuestra el extraordinario poder de Dios y los milagros imposibles.

programme.title: Ministry Now
programme.desc: La familia Lamb lleva a los espectadores de todo el mundo a Ministry Now. Lleno de fe radical, declaraciones alentadoras y consejos positivos.

programme.title: Andrés Spyker de Más Vida
programme.desc: Conéctate cada semana con un mensaje fresco de la palabra de Dios con una aplicación práctica para tu vida.

programme.title: Adoración en casa
programme.desc: Transforme la atmósfera de lo que sea que esté enfrentando a través del poder de la adoración con Josh Morales.

programme.title: Fe y Fuego
programme.desc: Jesús dijo, el que cree en mí, de su interior correrán ríos de agua viva. No importa la tribulación la que estamos, el Espíritu de Dios en nosotros es poderoso.

programme.title: Descubriendo al Jesús Judío
programme.desc: El rabino Schneider explora la obra continua del Espíritu Santo en la vida de cada creyente. Descubre cómo ser más consciente de la presencia de Dios.

programme.title: A Todo Gol
programme.desc: A Todo Gol une fútbol, fe y liderazgo. En el Mundial, invitados comparten análisis y reflexiones sobre valores de Dios fe y Cristo.

programme.title: The Potter's Touch with Bishop T.D. Jakes
programme.desc: Programa de entrevistas diario que aborda temas de actualidad relacionados con la sanidad. Presentado por el obispo TD Jakes.

programme.title: Jentezen Franklin
programme.desc: Encuentre respuestas en la Palabra de Dios y aprenda a vivir cada día con un propósito mientras Jentezen Franklin presenta la verdad clara que lo inspirará.

programme.title: Andrew Wommack La Verdad Del Evangelio
programme.desc: Andrew Wommack comparte cómo la Palabra de Dios transformó su vida. Él cree que el mismo poder puede obrar para ti.

programme.title: El cielo en la Tierra
programme.desc: Predicación de la Iglesia Cristiana "El Lugar de Su Presencia" en Bogotá, Colombia, Latinoamérica con temas aplicables a la vida diaria y consejos bíblicos.

programme.title: Pastor Sam Rodriguez
programme.desc: El pastor Samuel Rodriguez comparte la palabra del señor con enseñanzas, ilustraciones, y verdades que desatan la gloria de Dios en esta generación.

programme.title: Wenderholm & Scandretts Park: New Zealand
programme.desc: Scenery of scenic regional parks with their coastal forests and rocky bluffs that border spectacular ocean views.

programme.title: Campfire on the Beach
programme.desc: Nothing wraps up a beach day like the glow of a campfire at sunset, with waves rolling in the distance.

programme.title: Coral Kingdom
programme.desc: Arrangements of colorful coral sway to the rhythm of the tide, with aquatic visitors stopping by to enjoy the scenery.

programme.title: Underwater Wonders
programme.desc: Highlighting the abundant and varied flora and fauna that dwells underwater in several oceans around the world.

programme.title: Crystal Waterfalls of Croatia

programme.desc: Haast Pass, the peak home to these roaring falls of melted glacial waters. Mysteriously misty forests, gorgeous nature trails, and local wildlife.

programme.title: Flying Over Italy
programme.desc: Discovering the colorful architecture and stunning landscapes of Italy in a bird's-eye view voyage.

programme.title: Waimamaku Beach: New Zealand
programme.desc: Exploring the remote, cliff-lined, cavernous coastline of Waimamaku Beach.

programme.title: Yosemite Magic: Yosemite National Park
programme.desc: Yosemite is known all over the world for its granite cliffs, waterfalls, and giant sequoias.

programme.title: Kapas Biru Waterfall: Indonesia
programme.desc: Escape to Indonesia with tropical vegetation and a rushing waterfall.

programme.title: Journey Around the World
programme.desc: There is no better way to relax and unwind than traveling to some of the world's most remote locations.

programme.title: Coastal Windmill Relaxation
programme.desc: Taking viewers to two different South Australia Wind Farms; filmed at South Australia's Cape Jervis, overlooking Saint Vincent Gulf and Woakwine Range.

programme.title: Tropical beaches: Indonesia
programme.desc: A remote Indonesian island with breathtaking scenery of various beaches.

programme.title: Nusa Penida: Indonesia
programme.desc: A bird's eye view of Nusa Penida.

programme.title: Nature's Beauty: World of Relaxation
programme.desc: Scenes from over 100 different exotic, natural destinations around the world; a seamless, graciously flowing mix of several Nature Relaxation shorter length productions, designed to keep you in a state of complete relaxation from start to finish; transitioning you from golden sunsets to snowy mountaintops; from hidden canyon waterfalls to tropical islands so pure you've likely never seen anything like it!

programme.title: Kabut Pelangi Waterfall: Indonesia
programme.desc: Traveling to East Java and experiencing a waterfall tucked deep in the lush forest.

programme.title: Above the Fiji Islands
programme.desc: Featuring aerial views of remote South Pacific islands, accompanied by nature sounds and ambient music by Relax Moods; offering a mix of ground-level and bird's-eye perspectives through smooth, continuous shots, concluding with a real-time sunset over the sea.

programme.title: Flying Over the Atlantic Ocean
programme.desc: Travelling the coasts of the Atlantic Ocean from a bird's-eye view.

programme.title: New Zealand Gardens
programme.desc: The filmmaker explores the breathtaking gardens of New Zealand and provides the diversity and beauty of these enchanting havens.

programme.title: Bountiful Waterfalls: American North West
programme.desc: No need for a vacation to check out some of the most beautiful waterfalls of the American North West.

programme.title: Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: A look at a compilation of spectacular underwater images from around the world.

programme.title: Flow of Life
programme.desc: Features images of landscapes such as mountains, forests, creeks, rivers, butterflies, bees, bumblebees, lizards and more; includes gentle breath reminders to assist in a meditative experience; relaxing music composed by Chris Collins.

programme.desc: Feast your eyes on the diverse beauty of Sri Lanka, where rainforests, quiet beaches and vibrant foliage put you in a dreamy trance of natural wonder.

programme.desc: Viewers are oohed and aahed by stunning scenery from California (Big Sur, Yosemite), Hawaii, Utah, Argentina, Mexico, Tahiti, Oregon, and more.

programme.desc: Filmed along the eastern shores of Oahu, Hawaii; waves crash and glisten as the golden sun casts its warming rays.

programme.title: Fly Over Kauai, Hawaii
programme.desc: Experiencing Hawaii from above in a spectacular flight over stunning coastlines and lush landscapes.

programme.desc: Experiencing the tranquil waters of a laid-back marina tucked away in the Bahamas.

programme.desc: Discover the awe-inspiring waters of Indonesia with a tour of various vacation-worthy spots.

programme.title: Peaceful Farms: New Zealand
programme.desc: Calming music with animals quietly grazing the lush landscape.

programme.title: Lake Brunner & Paringa: New Zealand
programme.desc: Exploring a lakeside getaway.

programme.title: Fly Over Kelingking Beach: Indonesia
programme.desc: A bird's eye view of a scenic beach surrounded by pristine waters and high cliffs.

programme.title: Fly Over Paradise: Philippines
programme.desc: A journey to discover tropical beaches.

programme.title: Pantai Ngopet: Indonesia
programme.desc: Discover the awe-inspiring waters of Indonesia with a tour of various vacation-worthy spots.

programme.title: Yosemite Magic: Yosemite National Park
programme.desc: Yosemite is known all over the world for its granite cliffs, waterfalls, and giant sequoias.

programme.title: Rainbow Reef Relaxation 1
programme.desc: Vibrant underwater reefs, including many scenes which no longer look as depicted due to coral bleaching.

programme.title: Garden of Eden
programme.desc: Discover the world's natural beauty with flowers, butterflies, and waterfalls.

programme.title: Disappearing Beauty
programme.desc: From awe-inspiring wildlife to unbelievable landscapes, lose yourself in this compilation of mind-soothing footage-an ode to our planet's fleeting natural wonders.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: The true soul of Greece lies in the Peloponnese, a rugged peninsula where dramatic mountains have witnessed the rise and fall of civilizations for centuries.

programme.title: Concerto - OSN Marc Albrecht E Simone Lamsma Torino
programme.desc: In this 2022 concert in Turin, Marc Albrecht conducts the Orchestra Sinfonica Nazionale della Rai, with Simone Lamsma as the violin soloist. The programme includes Shostakovich's Violin Concerto No. 1, showcasing Lamsma's technical brilliance and emotional depth, and Franck's Symphony in D minor, celebrated for its rich harmonies and cyclical structure. The performance highlights a powerful blend of passion and precision.

programme.title: Med Whats On Extraordinary Events
programme.desc: Spring is transforming the Mediterranean into a massive stage for high-tech art and spectacular outdoor events. We begin at the Ta Qali National Park for the Bloom Festival, where wellness and music create a vibrant sanctuary in the heart of Malta. From there, we head into the tunnels and forts of Valletta and Birgu for the Malta Biennale 2026 to discover contemporary installations hidden inside historic sites rarely seen by the public. Our journey takes us to the cliffs of Gozo as giant creative designs fill the sky for the International Kite and Wind Festival before we land in Venice to witness the world class creativity of the 61st International Art Exhibition. This is a look at a region in the middle of a stunning cultural rebirth.

programme.title: MED Destination VENICE
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: Med Destination - Dubrovnik
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: MED Destination ANTIPAROS
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: Mediterranean Yoga Let go of tension
programme.desc: Mediterranean Yoga is a series that blends Embodiment Yoga with the peaceful beauty of the Mediterranean. Set in stunning coastal and natural locations, each episode encourages viewers to reconnect mind and body through gentle movement, breathwork, and mindfulness in a safe and calming space.

programme.title: Memoirs July 15
programme.desc: Memoirs July 15

programme.title: Whats On Music Festivals All Over the Med
programme.desc: The Mediterranean comes alive to the sound of music. From Malta high energy Rock the South and sophisticated Malta Jazz Festival, to Italy cutting edge Limbo Festival, Spain forward thinking Sonar Festival, and Turkey classical and contemporary Istanbul Music Festival, every corner of the region vibrates with rhythm, passion, and creativity.

programme.title: Med Minds 06 Jean de Valette
programme.desc: Jean Parisot de Valette, the steadfast Grand Master of Malta, led the Knights with courage, defended his island, and saved Europe as we know it.

programme.title: Med Nations 01 Italy
programme.desc: The heart of Italy! A place where old empires, the brilliance of the Renaissance, and modern passion all come together. Italy shows itself through its traditions, flavours, and lively way of life. From the timeless beauty of Rome to the artistic heart of Florence and the vibrant energy of Naples, Italy is alive with life. It's a journey through a country that has had a profound impact on art, cuisine, and culture throughout the Mediterranean and beyond.

programme.title: MED Destination ANTIPAROS
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: Med Destinations Lake Bled
programme.desc: Step into the fairytale beauty of Lake Bled, Slovenia, on Med Destinations. Crystal-clear waters, a picturesque island church, and lush Alpine surroundings make it a serene Mediterranean escape.

programme.title: Outdoor and Nature Ep 5
programme.desc: OUTDOOR & Nature, a weekly program that highlights sports, outdoor activities, and the breathtaking scenery of Cyprus, will introduce you to the great outdoors. Each episode highlights government initiatives, community organisations, and volunteer efforts to preserve Cyprus's natural heritage while examining nature, wildlife, and conservation. Take a trip of inspiration, exploration, and adventure with hosts Panicos Pilalis and Aristo Aristeidou. Listen and get motivated to go on your own outdoor adventure in Cyprus!

programme.title: Med Destinations Ancona
programme.desc: Explore Ancona, a bustling port city on the Adriatic, with Med Destinations. Discover its historic landmarks, scenic waterfronts, and the unique blend of maritime tradition and modern Italian life.

programme.title: Into the Med Gozo Island of Traditions
programme.desc: Born on the sunny island of Gozo, Susannah always had a connection to the Mediterranean. After moving to the Netherlands as a child, she is returning to the Mediterranean to rediscover her roots. Starting with the raw beauty of her home island, she invites you to explore the Mediterranean through a lens of discovery and belonging.

programme.title: Med Nations 01 Italy
programme.desc: The heart of Italy! A place where old empires, the brilliance of the Renaissance, and modern passion all come together. Italy shows itself through its traditions, flavours, and lively way of life. From the timeless beauty of Rome to the artistic heart of Florence and the vibrant energy of Naples, Italy is alive with life. It's a journey through a country that has had a profound impact on art, cuisine, and culture throughout the Mediterranean and beyond.

programme.title: Split (Firule)
programme.desc: In Split’s Firule neighborhood—home to Croatia’s tennis greats—we discovered more than just sport. Renowned chef Hrvoje Zirojević welcomed us with a taste of tradition: pivac na tingul, a classic Dalmatian stew made with tender young rooster.

programme.title: Med Destinations Lake Bled
programme.desc: Step into the fairytale beauty of Lake Bled, Slovenia, on Med Destinations. Crystal-clear waters, a picturesque island church, and lush Alpine surroundings make it a serene Mediterranean escape.

programme.title: MED Destination VENICE
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: Memoirs July 15
programme.desc: Memoirs July 15

programme.title: Malini Angelica Morocco MARRAKESH
programme.desc: Join Malini Angelica in Marrakesh, Morocco, as she dives into the heart of this ancient city—discovering its vibrant local life, rich history, and cultural heritage. Then, travel with her beyond the city walls to explore centuries-old kasbahs, including the legendary Ait Ben Haddou.

programme.title: Whats On Trade, Exhibitions and Unique Experiences
programme.desc: There is a unique energy that takes over the Mediterranean as the summer heat begins to build. This week, we are bypassing the usual tourist routes to find the heartbeat of the region through its most spectacular festivals. Imagine the sun setting over a lake in Tuscany as the Puccini Festival opens its 72nd season with the powerful melodies of Turandot. From that timeless elegance, we head straight into the high energy of Malta, where the Isle of MTV and the 10th anniversary of the Glitch Festival are turning the islands into a global hub for electronic music. We also explore the stunning heights of the Spanish Pyrenees for the floating stage at Pirineos Sur and hit the iconic cliffs of Santorini for the urban vibes of the Supa Dupa Fly Festival. This is a journey through a region that knows exactly how to celebrate its past while dancing firmly into the future.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: Here along the Côte d’Azur, we wander the sunlit promenades of Nice, catch whispers of fortunes changing hands at Monaco’s famed casino, and step inside the elegant villas once home to artists and heiresses.

programme.title: Med Nations 07 Croatia
programme.desc: Sail along the stunning Adriatic coast of Croatia. From the medieval streets of Dubrovnik to sunlit islands and crystal-clear waters, Croatia reveals a rich tapestry of history, culture, and seaside traditions that make it a Mediterranean gem.

programme.title: Mediterranean Yoga Move and feel Awake
programme.desc: Mediterranean Yoga is a series that blends Embodiment Yoga with the peaceful beauty of the Mediterranean. Set in stunning coastal and natural locations, each episode encourages viewers to reconnect mind and body through gentle movement, breathwork, and mindfulness in a safe and calming space.

programme.title: Med Minds 06 Jean de Valette
programme.desc: Jean Parisot de Valette, the steadfast Grand Master of Malta, led the Knights with courage, defended his island, and saved Europe as we know it.

programme.title: Azzurro Storie di mare: Alla scoperta di Ischia, isola verde
programme.desc: A journey along Italy’s coast, from Ponza to Calabria, exploring its natural beauty and the people who protect it. Fishermen, divers, and activists share their passion for the sea and their fight against pollution.

programme.title: Med Destinations Crete
programme.desc: Med Destinations takes you to Crete, the jewel of the Greek islands, where ancient history meets breathtaking landscapes. Explore charming villages, hidden beaches, and the vibrant local culture that makes Crete a must-visit Mediterranean paradise.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: With its thought-provoking sights, delicious cuisine, and idyllic coastline, it's no wonder Normandy is both a Mecca for history buffs and a popular getaway for Parisians.

programme.title: Whats On Music Festivals All Over the Med
programme.desc: The Mediterranean comes alive to the sound of music. From Malta high energy Rock the South and sophisticated Malta Jazz Festival, to Italy cutting edge Limbo Festival, Spain forward thinking Sonar Festival, and Turkey classical and contemporary Istanbul Music Festival, every corner of the region vibrates with rhythm, passion, and creativity.

programme.desc: Malini Angelica heads to Alexandria, Egypt’s Mediterranean city, exploring its blend of Greek, Roman, and Egyptian history. With a lively student scene, fresh seafood, and a relaxed seaside vibe, she discovers why Alexandria is an underrated, modern-feeling escape.

programme.title: Med Destinations Lake Bled

programme.title: Gold Cuisine

programme.title: Med Nations 07 Croatia
programme.desc: Sail along the stunning Adriatic coast of Croatia. From the medieval streets of Dubrovnik to sunlit islands and crystal-clear waters, Croatia reveals a rich tapestry of history, culture, and seaside traditions that make it a Mediterranean gem.

programme.title: Mediterranean Kitchen France
programme.desc: Chef Kurt invites Chef Geoffrey into the kitchen to cook a brilliant edible art show: delicate French pastries intricately designed to mimic Malta’s iconic architecture. As they sculpt and bake, the conversation centres on precision, the discipline of pastry work, and how travel inspires creative fusion. It’s a visually stunning masterclass that proves food can be a bridge between two cultures.

programme.title: Med Destinations Pula
programme.desc: Discover Pula, Croatia, with Med Destinations. Famous for its Roman amphitheater and charming old town, Pula combines historical grandeur with Adriatic seaside beauty.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: For many travelers, the quintessence of Spain is found in Andalucía. Join us in Granada, Córdoba, and the Costa del Sol as we enjoy the food, history, art, and dance of Europe's most passionate corner.

programme.title: MED Destination ANTIPAROS
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: Med Destinations Crete
programme.desc: Med Destinations takes you to Crete, the jewel of the Greek islands, where ancient history meets breathtaking landscapes. Explore charming villages, hidden beaches, and the vibrant local culture that makes Crete a must-visit Mediterranean paradise.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Calabria
programme.desc: Malini explores Calabria, a hidden gem in Southern Italy. While its coastal towns like Tropea and Scilla boast turquoise seas, Calabria’s true charm lies in its rural interior, where traditions like handmade pasta, cheese, and salami are still alive. She visits ghost villages like Pentedattilo in the Aspromonte mountains and experiences authentic Calabrian culture, food, and hospitality, hosted by Calabria Etnica.

programme.title: IL Canale du Midi dall Atlantico al Mediterraneo
programme.desc: Peoples, distant lands, and lost civilizations come to life with Alberto Angela. New discoveries unfold with a fresh set design and two new features: a diary from the Pompeii excavations and visits to major art exhibitions in Italy’s museums.

programme.title: Med Destinations Ios
programme.desc: Discover the lively spirit and scenic beauty of Ios on Med Destinations. From crystal-clear beaches to winding streets and sunset views, Ios combines vibrant island life with the tranquility of the Aegean Sea.

programme.title: Dates that made history
programme.desc: Mansa Musa's Pilgrimage to Mecca explores the extraordinary journey of Mansa Musa, the emperor of Mali, to Mecca in 1324. Highlighting the wealth and power of the Mali Empire as Musa’s caravan traverses the Sahara, spreading wealth and religious influence. It examines the impact of his pilgrimage on both the Islamic world and Africa’s place in global history. Through this journey, Mansa Musa’s legacy of generosity and cultural exchange becomes legendary.

programme.title: Great Myths the Iliad
programme.desc: Telling the story of Homer’s Iliad, the epic poem of Ancient Greece recounts the Trojan War with its mortal heroes and meddling gods. In this episode Helen’s husband Menelaus and her lover Paris face each other in a duel.

programme.title: Mediterranean Yoga Slowdown For Bed
programme.desc: Mediterranean Yoga is a series that blends Embodiment Yoga with the peaceful beauty of the Mediterranean. Set in stunning coastal and natural locations, each episode encourages viewers to reconnect mind and body through gentle movement, breathwork, and mindfulness in a safe and calming space.

programme.title: Concerto per Milano 2022
programme.desc: The Filarmonica della Scala and principal conductor Riccardo Chailly return to Piazza Duomo for the evocative Concerto per Milano. A landmark cultural event, this open-air performance brings world-class music to the heart of the city. Television direction by Stefania Grimaldi captures the grandeur and emotion of the evening.

programme.title: Whats On Trade, Exhibitions and Unique Experiences
programme.desc: There is a unique energy that takes over the Mediterranean as the summer heat begins to build. This week, we are bypassing the usual tourist routes to find the heartbeat of the region through its most spectacular festivals. Imagine the sun setting over a lake in Tuscany as the Puccini Festival opens its 72nd season with the powerful melodies of Turandot. From that timeless elegance, we head straight into the high energy of Malta, where the Isle of MTV and the 10th anniversary of the Glitch Festival are turning the islands into a global hub for electronic music. We also explore the stunning heights of the Spanish Pyrenees for the floating stage at Pirineos Sur and hit the iconic cliffs of Santorini for the urban vibes of the Supa Dupa Fly Festival. This is a journey through a region that knows exactly how to celebrate its past while dancing firmly into the future.

programme.title: Outdoor and Nature

programme.title: Brač (Supetar)

programme.desc: From the Rai Auditorium in Turin, the Rai National Symphony Orchestra, conducted by Marcello Rota and featuring soprano Cristina Mosca, celebrates 100 years since the birth of Fellini. programme.title: Kaštela (U masliniku)
programme.desc: In Kaštela, a cluster of seven towns near Split, winemaking runs deep. Among olive groves and vineyards, Mladen Kuzmanić treated us to local flavors—sipa i bob, pancetta, goat cheese, and homemade sausage—perfectly paired with a glass of regional wine.

programme.title: Med Destination - Dubrovnik
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: Malini Angelica Amalfi
programme.desc: Malini Angelica travels to Italy’s stunning Amalfi Coast, exploring its dramatic cliffs, colorful seaside towns, and crystal-clear waters. From the charm of Positano and Amalfi to the tranquility of Ravello, she experiences the region’s beauty, cuisine, and coastal lifestyle. Malini captures the magic that makes the Amalfi Coast one of Italy’s most breathtaking destinations.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: Join Rick as we explore Andalucía - prancing horses, whitewashed hilltowns, kite surfing on sunny beaches. Then we'll summit the Rock of Gibraltar and sample Morocco, from tribal musicians and busy artisans to the magic of its markets

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Luxurious hotels, thrilling water parks, inexpensive food and almost 300 days of bright sunny weather make Bulgaria’s biggest seaside resort, Sunny Beach popular for budget beach getaways. Catriona, however, stumbles onto the historic town of Nessebar, which is over 3000 years old, and realizes that there is more to the place than meets the eye. She goes on to visit the Castra Rubra winery, where she finds out about the history of Viticulture in Bulgaria and the process of wine making.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Benafsha embarks on a whirlwind adventure in Ahmedabad. With just 48 hours in the city, she is determined to make the most of it, but how?

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From exploring a vintage car museum to scaling a 5 meter ice-wall and sampling a rich bread cookie, Watch Alex exploring The Hague, also known as the “International city of peace”.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Požarevac and visits the ancient town of Viminacium. Later visits National Museum, concluding with Majdanpek’s Rajkova Pecina Caves.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Rovaniemi, where she visits a husky farm and finds out about about breeding and raising huskies.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: For the people of Sofia, preserving its heritage is sacrosanct, and this can be seen in the way the city's infrastructure is built around its archeological treasures. Catriona realizes this when she roams around the cobblestone streets of the city. With every monument she visits, she finds herself transported to a different era, right from Perperikon fort, which narrate stories of the Byzantine empire, to Boyana church, which is home to some of the earliest examples of medieval artwork.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: From lazing in hammocks by the beach to sipping drinks on a private yacht, Kim realizes that Maldives is the perfect destination to unwind in style.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying dishes like Stroopwafel and Palat Stoofvlees to visiting the Erasmus bridge, cube houses and wall painting on the streets, Alex explores Rotterdam.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Since medieval times Aberdeenshire and its northwestern neighbour Moray have been the richest and most fertile regions of the Highlands. Aberdeenshire is famed for its Aberdeen Angus beef cattle, its many fine castles and the prosperous 'granite city' of Aberdeen.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Explore Majdanpek with our host as the city brings the viewers closer to the treasures strewn across the Serbian countryside.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Levi is Finland's largest and fastest-growing ski destination. Emeline is here, to find out the exciting activities that Levi offers, in summer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: With snow-capped peaks rising over primeval forests and traditional villages, Paro is like an Oriental Switzerland. On a visit to Paro, Radhika explores famous monasteries such as the Rinpung Dzong and Takstang Monastery, and learns the history and legends associated with them. Along the way, she finds out the secrets to the quintessential Bhutanese peace and contentment.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: An invigorating spa treatment, a cooking lesson, a snorkeling session – Kim has experiences in the Maldives that leave her completely rejuvenated.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying Orange zest beer and traditional Dutch dishes to exploring St.Martin's Cathedral, Dom Tower and enjoying go karting and stand up paddle boat ride, watch Alex enjoy his trip in Utrecht.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: In the enchanting landscapes of Inverness, Robbie’s journey takes a whimsical turn: from eccentric to downright magical.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Nis,one of the oldest cities in Europe.She visits a concentration camp and later paraglides to end by watching a musical performance.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline goes to Ruka-Kuusamo, where she tries river rafting. She also meets a reindeer herder,who tells her about the unique ways of herding reindeer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Actress and travel enthusiast, Radhika Bangia explores the enigma that is Bhutan, when she visits Thimpu, the capital city with no traffic lights and no skyscrapers invading the skyline. Here, she checks out significant sights such as the Tibetan-style National Memorial Chorten, the National Library and the Folk Heritage Museum in order to understand more about the beliefs, history, architecture and cuisine of Bhutan.

programme.title: World Heritage
programme.desc: Explore the ancient heritage sites of Abu Simbel, the Philae complex of Nubia and the Hatshepsut Temple to unravel the mystical stories behind the ancient temples of Egypt.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex tries traditional Dutch food in Leiden, learns about Dutch history at Prinsenhof Museum, visits a Gothic church, a local cheese market and Delftware factory at Gouda.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: The grand finale of Robbie’s wild homecoming unfolds amidst the rugged beauty of the Isle of Skye.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Continue exploring Serbia with Heather as she visits the Manasija monastery, Resavska caves, ending with a joyride onboard Eight of Sargan rail line.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: Krisann and Nathan’s vacation in Mauritius begins in Le Morne, where an unexpected adventure awaits the latter.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Known as Druk Yul or the “Land of the Thunder Dragon”, Bhutan is the country of mountains, monasteries and mystery. Radhika Bangia finds this to be true when she visits Punakha, the former capital of Bhutan, where she marvels at the architecture of Punakha Dzong and gets to know the story behind the two rivers, Mo Chhu and Po Chhu. She also explores the sacred temples and Mebar Tsho lake of Bumthang, the religious heart of Bhutan, and gets to know the local legends.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries out spa treatments, which are inspired by the Dead Sea and the desert surroundings at Hotel Kempinski Ishtar in Jordan.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: After Burj Khalifa, Alex has a traditional Emirati platter, visits the gold market and skiing rink, and goes for a camel safari and camping.

programme.title: City break
programme.desc: Nestled in the mighty Alps, tour the Austrian city of Innsbruck with Sabrina and explore a town that’s alpine-urban yet traditional in nature.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: In her final leg of journey, Heather visits the Uvac canyon, tries locally prepared pie, ending with Kopaonik National Park and Jelovarnik waterfalls.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: On an island-hopping adventure in Mauritius, Krisann and Nathan find out that time works differently on these islands.

programme.title: XP Guide
programme.desc: A homely place where the aroma of freshly baked bread greets you as people smoothly glide along on their bicycles. Welcome to Copenhagen.

programme.title: Unwind
programme.desc: Indeya explores the Cinnamon plantations, tries a local dish and witnesses a traditional dance performance.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex has varied experiences in Dubai like trying Lebanese cheese bread, visiting a coffee museum, going to IMG Worlds of Adventure and more.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: In Amsterdam, Alex tries Bitterballen, cannabis cake and visits Heineken Experience brewery. He explores Zaanse Schans village with windmills and Coster Diamonds, the oldest diamond polishing factory.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s journey to reconnect with his Scottish roots begins in Edinburgh, the stunning capital city perched on extinct volcanoes. Navigating the twists of the Royal Mile, strutting around the majestic Edinburgh castle and learning to sip scotch like a true Scotsman, he feels a tad closer to his heritage. The real fun kicks in, however, when he stumbles onto a rather flamboyant way to unleash his inner Highlander.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Like Rome, Plovdiv straddles seven hills; but as Europe’s oldest continuously inhabited city, it’s far more ancient. As Catriona takes a tour of the city to see its iconic attractions such as a Roman Amphitheatre, which was once the setting for gladiator fights, and the striking Baroque Saints Constantine and Helena Church, she begins to see how seamlessly modern life blends with the age-old ruins. Along the way, she realizes why Plovdiv is called the ‘Eternal City’ of Bulgaria.

programme.title: XP Guide
programme.desc: Stockholm, the capital and largest city of Sweden, was built on 14 Islands. This place is also sometimes referred as Venice of the north.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: This exhilarating city of castles, beer and segway rides can take you on a break that will truly leave you catching your breath.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Visit Belgrade, with Heather as she explores the city, its architecture and learns about its past by visiting historical monuments.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: In Amsterdam, Alex tries Bitterballen, cannabis cake and visits Heineken Experience brewery. He explores Zaanse Schans village with windmills and Coster Diamonds, the oldest diamond polishing factory.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s journey to reconnect with his Scottish roots begins in Edinburgh, the stunning capital city perched on extinct volcanoes. Navigating the twists of the Royal Mile, strutting around the majestic Edinburgh castle and learning to sip scotch like a true Scotsman, he feels a tad closer to his heritage. The real fun kicks in, however, when he stumbles onto a rather flamboyant way to unleash his inner Highlander.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Like Rome, Plovdiv straddles seven hills; but as Europe’s oldest continuously inhabited city, it’s far more ancient. As Catriona takes a tour of the city to see its iconic attractions such as a Roman Amphitheatre, which was once the setting for gladiator fights, and the striking Baroque Saints Constantine and Helena Church, she begins to see how seamlessly modern life blends with the age-old ruins. Along the way, she realizes why Plovdiv is called the ‘Eternal City’ of Bulgaria.

But the real thunder of the Highlands awaits at the Braemar Games gathering, where tartan meets brawn, and Robbie learns that embracing his Scottish side involves more than just kilts and castles.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Novi Sad, the financial and industrial hub of Serbia.Starting with canoeing on Danube and later exploring the city and its landmarks.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Santa Village at Rovaniemi, where she reads some of the letters that children from around the world write to him.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Luxurious hotels, thrilling water parks, inexpensive food and almost 300 days of bright sunny weather make Bulgaria’s biggest seaside resort, Sunny Beach popular for budget beach getaways. Catriona, however, stumbles onto the historic town of Nessebar, which is over 3000 years old, and realizes that there is more to the place than meets the eye. She goes on to visit the Castra Rubra winery, where she finds out about the history of Viticulture in Bulgaria and the process of wine making.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Benafsha embarks on a whirlwind adventure in Ahmedabad. With just 48 hours in the city, she is determined to make the most of it, but how?

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From exploring a vintage car museum to scaling a 5 meter ice-wall and sampling a rich bread cookie, Watch Alex exploring The Hague, also known as the “International city of peace”.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Požarevac and visits the ancient town of Viminacium. Later visits National Museum, concluding with Majdanpek’s Rajkova Pecina Caves.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Rovaniemi, where she visits a husky farm and finds out about about breeding and raising huskies.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: For the people of Sofia, preserving its heritage is sacrosanct, and this can be seen in the way the city's infrastructure is built around its archeological treasures. Catriona realizes this when she roams around the cobblestone streets of the city. With every monument she visits, she finds herself transported to a different era, right from Perperikon fort, which narrate stories of the Byzantine empire, to Boyana church, which is home to some of the earliest examples of medieval artwork.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: From lazing in hammocks by the beach to sipping drinks on a private yacht, Kim realizes that Maldives is the perfect destination to unwind in style.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying dishes like Stroopwafel and Palat Stoofvlees to visiting the Erasmus bridge, cube houses and wall painting on the streets, Alex explores Rotterdam.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Since medieval times Aberdeenshire and its northwestern neighbour Moray have been the richest and most fertile regions of the Highlands. Aberdeenshire is famed for its Aberdeen Angus beef cattle, its many fine castles and the prosperous 'granite city' of Aberdeen.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Explore Majdanpek with our host as the city brings the viewers closer to the treasures strewn across the Serbian countryside.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Levi is Finland's largest and fastest-growing ski destination. Emeline is here, to find out the exciting activities that Levi offers, in summer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: With snow-capped peaks rising over primeval forests and traditional villages, Paro is like an Oriental Switzerland. On a visit to Paro, Radhika explores famous monasteries such as the Rinpung Dzong and Takstang Monastery, and learns the history and legends associated with them. Along the way, she finds out the secrets to the quintessential Bhutanese peace and contentment.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: An invigorating spa treatment, a cooking lesson, a snorkeling session – Kim has experiences in the Maldives that leave her completely rejuvenated.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying Orange zest beer and traditional Dutch dishes to exploring St.Martin's Cathedral, Dom Tower and enjoying go karting and stand up paddle boat ride, watch Alex enjoy his trip in Utrecht.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: In the enchanting landscapes of Inverness, Robbie’s journey takes a whimsical turn: from eccentric to downright magical.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Nis,one of the oldest cities in Europe.She visits a concentration camp and later paraglides to end by watching a musical performance.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline goes to Ruka-Kuusamo, where she tries river rafting. She also meets a reindeer herder,who tells her about the unique ways of herding reindeer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Actress and travel enthusiast, Radhika Bangia explores the enigma that is Bhutan, when she visits Thimpu, the capital city with no traffic lights and no skyscrapers invading the skyline. Here, she checks out significant sights such as the Tibetan-style National Memorial Chorten, the National Library and the Folk Heritage Museum in order to understand more about the beliefs, history, architecture and cuisine of Bhutan.

programme.title: World Heritage
programme.desc: Explore the ancient heritage sites of Abu Simbel, the Philae complex of Nubia and the Hatshepsut Temple to unravel the mystical stories behind the ancient temples of Egypt.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex tries traditional Dutch food in Leiden, learns about Dutch history at Prinsenhof Museum, visits a Gothic church, a local cheese market and Delftware factory at Gouda.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: The grand finale of Robbie’s wild homecoming unfolds amidst the rugged beauty of the Isle of Skye.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Continue exploring Serbia with Heather as she visits the Manasija monastery, Resavska caves, ending with a joyride onboard Eight of Sargan rail line.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: Krisann and Nathan’s vacation in Mauritius begins in Le Morne, where an unexpected adventure awaits the latter.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Known as Druk Yul or the “Land of the Thunder Dragon”, Bhutan is the country of mountains, monasteries and mystery. Radhika Bangia finds this to be true when she visits Punakha, the former capital of Bhutan, where she marvels at the architecture of Punakha Dzong and gets to know the story behind the two rivers, Mo Chhu and Po Chhu. She also explores the sacred temples and Mebar Tsho lake of Bumthang, the religious heart of Bhutan, and gets to know the local legends.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries out spa treatments, which are inspired by the Dead Sea and the desert surroundings at Hotel Kempinski Ishtar in Jordan.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: After Burj Khalifa, Alex has a traditional Emirati platter, visits the gold market and skiing rink, and goes for a camel safari and camping.

programme.title: City break
programme.desc: Nestled in the mighty Alps, tour the Austrian city of Innsbruck with Sabrina and explore a town that’s alpine-urban yet traditional in nature.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: In her final leg of journey, Heather visits the Uvac canyon, tries locally prepared pie, ending with Kopaonik National Park and Jelovarnik waterfalls.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: On an island-hopping adventure in Mauritius, Krisann and Nathan find out that time works differently on these islands.

programme.title: XP Guide
programme.desc: A homely place where the aroma of freshly baked bread greets you as people smoothly glide along on their bicycles. Welcome to Copenhagen.

programme.title: Unwind
programme.desc: Indeya explores the Cinnamon plantations, tries a local dish and witnesses a traditional dance performance.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex has varied experiences in Dubai like trying Lebanese cheese bread, visiting a coffee museum, going to IMG Worlds of Adventure and more.
